scenario: Meridian Logistics calls a child process from 7000 shipments per day in Camunda 8.8. The team wants every variable created inside the child process to flow up into the parent scope on completion, without authoring an output mapping for each individual variable on the call activity element.
question: Which call activity property propagates every child variable to the parent scope on completion in Camunda 8?

options.text: Enable propagateAllChildVariables on the call activity; every variable from the completed child instance is written to the parent scope
Set the implementation type to merge-child on the call activity; the engine writes every child variable into the parent scope on completion
Set the propagate-mode header to all on the call activity; the engine reads the custom header and propagates every child variable
Set inputCollection to all on the call activity; the engine treats the option as a wildcard for every variable in the child scope

a.text: Correct. Camunda 8 call activities support propagateAllChildVariables to copy every child variable to the parent on completion without per-variable output mappings.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no merge-child implementation type; the documented mechanism is the propagateAllChildVariables boolean property.

c.text: Incorrect. Custom headers are static metadata on the activity, not a propagation switch for child variables.

d.text: Incorrect. inputCollection belongs to multi-instance markers and applies to per-iteration inputs, not to call activity variable propagation.

explanation: Call activities expose propagateAllChildVariables to copy every variable from the completed child instance back to the parent scope automatically.
